howto_content.getting_started_content:
Welcome to BeamSkin Studio!

BeamSkin Studio is a professional tool for creating and managing vehicle skins for BeamNG.drive. This chapter will help you get started quickly.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“Ӣ What You Need
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

1. BeamNG.drive installed on your computer
2. An image editor that supports DDS format:
   вҖў Paint.NET (Free) - Recommended for beginners
   вҖў GIMP (Free) with DDS plugin
   вҖў Adobe Photoshop (Paid) with DDS plugin
   вҖў Substance Painter (Paid) - Professional option

3. Your custom texture ready to save as DDS

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺҜ Quick Start Guide
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Step 1: Create your texture
вҖў Design your skin in your image editor
вҖў Match the vehicle's UV map dimensions
вҖў Save as DDS format (BC3/DXT5 compression recommended)

Step 2: Name your file correctly
вҖў Format: carid_skin_YourSkinName.dds
вҖў Example: etk800_skin_RacingBlue.dds
вҖў Use the Car List tab to find the correct carid

Step 3: Add to project
вҖў Go to the Project tab
вҖў Add your vehicle to the project
вҖў Add your skin file to the vehicle
вҖў Fill in mod info

Step 4: Generate mod
вҖў Select your output location
вҖў Click "Generate Mod"
вҖў Launch BeamNG.drive and activate your mod

That's it! Your skin is now ready to use in-game.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’Ў Pro Tips
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вң“ Always save your project files (.bsproject) to avoid losing work
вң“ Keep original PSD/XCF files separate from DDS exports
вң“ Test skins in-game before sharing with others
вң“ Use descriptive names for easy organization
вң“ Back up your work regularly

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҡ пёҸ Common Beginner Mistakes
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вң— Using PNG or JPG instead of DDS format
вң— Incorrect file naming (missing "skin" or wrong carid)
вң— Forgetting to activate mod in BeamNG.drive
вң— Using spaces in file names
вң— Not restarting BeamNG after adding mod

Continue to the next chapter for detailed instructions on each feature.
howto_content.skin_creation_content:
Creating Professional Vehicle Skins

This chapter covers the technical requirements and best practices for creating high-quality vehicle skins.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“җ DDS File Requirements
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Required format: DDS (DirectDraw Surface)

Compression settings:
вҖў BC3 (DXT5) - Best for color + alpha
вҖў BC1 (DXT1) - For solid colors without transparency
вҖў BC7 - Highest quality

Recommended resolutions:
1:1 Aspect Ratio:
вҖў Performance quality: 4096 x 4096
вҖў Standard quality: 8192 x 8192
вҖў High quality: 16384 x 16384

2:1 Aspect Ratio:
вҖў Performance quality: 4096 x 2048
вҖў Standard quality: 8192 x 4096
вҖў High quality: 16384 x 8192

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“қ File Naming Convention
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Format: [carid]_skin_[SkinName].dds

Components:
1. carid - Vehicle identifier (find in Car List tab)
   вҖў Must be exact match
   вҖў Case-sensitive
   вҖў No spaces allowed

2. skin - Literal text "skin"
   вҖў Must be lowercase
   вҖў Required separator

3. SkinName - Your skin's unique name
   вҖў One word (no spaces)
   вҖў Letters and numbers only
   вҖў CamelCase recommended for readability

вң“ CORRECT examples:
вҖў etk800_skin_RacingStripes.dds
вҖў pickup_skin_Muddy.dds
вҖў sunburst2_skin_Police.dds

вң— INCORRECT examples:
вҖў etk 800_skin_Racing.dds (space in carid)
вҖў etk800_Racing.dds (missing "skin")
вҖў etk800_skin_Racing Stripes.dds (space in name)
вҖў etk800_paint_Racing.dds (wrong separator)

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺЁ Getting UV Maps
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

UV maps show where different parts of the car are on the texture.

Method 1: Using BeamSkin Studio (Recommended)
1. Go to the Car List tab
2. Find your vehicle
3. Click the "Get UV Map" button
4. Save the extracted template
5. Open in your image editor as a guide layer

Method 2: Manual extraction
1. Navigate to your BeamNG installation:
   C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\BeamNG.drive\content\vehicles\
2. Find the vehicle folder (e.g. "etk800")
3. Open the DDS texture in your editor
4. Save as template/reference

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ–ҢпёҸ Design Best Practices
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Layer organization:
вҖў Keep UV map as bottom reference layer
вҖў Organize design in folders/groups
вҖў Use adjustment layers for easy color changes
вҖў Maintain non-destructive workflow

Detail considerations:
вҖў Higher resolution areas = visible details (hood, doors)
вҖў Lower resolution areas = less important (undercarriage)
вҖў Add aging and wear effects for realism
вҖў Consider how light will affect the paint

Color management:
вҖў Use sRGB color space
вҖў Avoid pure black (use dark gray instead)
вҖў Test colors in different lighting conditions
вҖў Consider color blindness accessibility

Performance:
вҖў Don't use unnecessarily high resolutions
вҖў Compress textures appropriately
вҖў Test performance in-game
вҖў Balance quality against file size

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’ҫ Saving Your Work
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Workflow:
1. Save master file (.psd, .xcf, .kra)
   вҖў Keep all layers
   вҖў Easy to modify later
   вҖў High-quality source

2. Export to DDS
   вҖў Flatten layers
   вҖў Apply compression
   вҖў Save with correct naming

3. Keep organized backups
   вҖў Separate folders for each vehicle
   вҖў Version numbers for iterations
   вҖў Archive completed projects

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вңЁ Quality Checklist
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Before export:
вҳҗ UV alignment is correct
вҳҗ No visible seams or stretching
вҳҗ Resolution is appropriate
вҳҗ Colors look good in neutral light
вҳҗ Details are sharp and clear
вҳҗ File name follows convention
вҳҗ DDS compression is optimal
вҳҗ Mip maps are generated
вҳҗ Master file saved
вҳҗ Ready for in-game testing
howto_content.project_content:
Using the Project Tab

The Project tab is your main workspace for creating skin mods. It uses a project-based system that lets you manage multiple vehicles and skins in one place.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ—ӮпёҸ Project System Overview
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

BeamSkin Studio uses a multi-car, multi-skin system:

вҖў One project = One mod ZIP file
вҖў Each project can contain multiple vehicles
вҖў Each vehicle can have multiple skins
вҖў Projects can be saved and loaded
вҖў All skins export together into a single mod

Benefits:
вң“ Organize related skins together
вң“ Manage large skin collections easily
вң“ Save work in progress
вң“ Reuse projects for updates

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“қ Project Information
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Mod Name (ZIP name):
вҖў Becomes the filename of your mod
вҖў One word, no spaces
вҖў No special characters
вҖў Examples: MyCoolSkins, RacingPack, PolicePack
вҖў This name appears in your mods folder

Author Name:
вҖў Your name or username
вҖў Appears in all skin metadata
вҖў Spaces and special characters allowed
вҖў Displayed in BeamNG.drive skin selector

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҡ— Adding Vehicles to Project
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Step-by-step process:

1. Click "Select vehicle"
   вҖў Opens vehicle selector dialog
   вҖў Shows all available vehicles

2. Find your vehicle
   вҖў Search by name or ID
   вҖў Use filters if available
   вҖў Hover for preview images

3. Select the vehicle
   вҖў Click on the vehicle card
   вҖў Verify it is correct
   вҖў Check that carid matches your DDS file

4. Add to project
   вҖў Click "Add car to project"
   вҖў Vehicle appears in "Vehicles in project" section
   вҖў Ready to receive skins

Multiple instances:
вҖў You can add the same vehicle multiple times
вҖў Useful for organizing different skin themes
вҖў Example: "ETK 800 - Racing" and "ETK 800 - Casual"

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺЁ Adding Skins to Vehicles
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Process:

1. Select target vehicle
   вҖў Click a vehicle card in your project
   вҖў Card highlights to show it is selected
   вҖў Only one vehicle can be selected at a time

2. Enter skin name
   вҖў This is the display name shown in-game
   вҖў Spaces and special characters allowed
   вҖў Should be descriptive and unique
   вҖў Examples: "Racing Stripes Red", "Police Livery"

3. Browse for DDS file
   вҖў Click the "Browse" button
   вҖў Navigate to your skin file
   вҖў Select the correctly named DDS file
   вҖў File path displays in the field

4. Add the skin
   вҖў Click "Add skin to selected vehicle"
   вҖў Skin appears under the vehicle
   вҖў Can add more skins to same vehicle
   вҖў Repeat for all your skins

Skin management:
вҖў View all skins under each vehicle
вҖў Remove individual skins if needed
вҖў Reorder skins (if supported)
вҖў Preview which skins are included

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’ҫ Project Management
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Save project:
вҖў Saves current state to .bsproject file
вҖў Choose location and filename
вҖў Includes all vehicles and skins
вҖў Preserves all settings
вҖў Lets you continue working later

Load project:
вҖў Opens a saved .bsproject file
вҖў Restores all vehicles and skins
вҖў Checks if DDS files still exist
вҖў Warns about missing files
вҖў Continue where you left off

Clear project:
вҖў Removes all vehicles and skins
вҖў Starts fresh
вҖў Asks for confirmation
вҖў Cannot be undone
вҖў Project info is preserved

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“Ө Output Configuration
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Steam Workshop location (Default):
вҖў Automatic path detection
вҖў Windows: C:\Users\[username]\AppData\Local\BeamNG.drive\current\mods
вҖў Linux: ~/.local/share/BeamNG.drive/current/mods
вҖў macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/BeamNG.drive/current/mods
вҖў Mod activates automatically in-game

Custom location:
вҖў Choose any folder you want
вҖў Useful for manual management
вҖў Great for testing before sharing
вҖў Organize mods by category
вҖў Browse to select folder

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺҜ Generating Your Mod
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Before generating:
вҳҗ All vehicles have at least one skin
вҳҗ All DDS files are correctly named
вҳҗ Mod name is unique and valid
вҳҗ Author name is filled in
вҳҗ Output location is correct

Generation process:
1. Click "Generate Mod"
2. Tool processes all files
3. Creates folder structure
4. Copies all skin files
5. Generates metadata
6. Creates ZIP file
7. Moves to output location

Success indicators:
вң“ Success message appears
вң“ ZIP file created in output folder
вң“ No error messages
вң“ File size seems reasonable
вң“ Ready to test in-game

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ”§ Testing Your Mod
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

In BeamNG.drive:
1. Launch the game
2. Go to Content Manager > Mods
3. Find your mod in the list
4. Make sure it is enabled (checkbox)
5. Restart the game if needed
6. Spawn a vehicle
7. Check the color menu for your skins
8. Test each skin to verify appearance

Troubleshooting:
вҖў If skins don't appear, check file names
вҖў Make sure mod is enabled in Content Manager
вҖў Restart BeamNG.drive completely
вҖў Check mods folder for ZIP file
вҖў Verify carid matches the vehicle

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’Ў Workflow Tips
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Efficient organization:
вҖў One project per theme/collection
вҖў Group related skins together
вҖў Use descriptive mod names
вҖў Save projects often
вҖў Keep DDS files organized in folders

Version control:
вҖў Increment version numbers
вҖў Save different project versions
вҖў Keep notes on changes
вҖў Archive old versions
вҖў Document updates for users

Quality assurance:
вҖў Test each skin before adding more
вҖў Verify file names are correct
вҖў Check in different lighting conditions
вҖў Get feedback from others
вҖў Iterate and improve
howto_content.car_list_content:
Using the Car List Tab

The Car List tab provides a searchable database of all available vehicles, their IDs and tools for working with them.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ”Қ Finding Vehicles
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Search functionality:
вҖў Search by vehicle name (e.g. "Moonhawk")
вҖў Search by car ID (e.g. "moonhawk")
вҖў Case-insensitive search
вҖў Real-time results as you type
вҖў Partial matches supported

Browse mode:
вҖў Browse through the entire vehicle list
вҖў Sorted alphabetically
вҖў Visual card layout
вҖў Hover for preview images
вҖў Quick overview of all vehicles

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҸ·пёҸ Understanding Car IDs
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

What is a car ID?
вҖў Unique identifier for each vehicle
вҖў Used in file names and mod structure
вҖў Usually lowercase
вҖў May contain underscores
вҖў Never contains spaces

Common ID patterns:
вҖў Manufacturer_Model: "gavril_roamer"
вҖў Series: "etk800", "etkc"
вҖў Generation: "pickup" (D-Series), "midsize" (Pessima)
вҖў Specialty: "racetruck", "rockbouncer"

Finding the right ID:
1. Search for vehicle name in Car List
2. Vehicle card shows both name and ID
3. Use "Copy ID" button for accuracy
4. ID shown in carid field
5. Use this exact ID in your DDS file name

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“Ӣ Vehicle Info Cards
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Each vehicle card shows:
вҖў Vehicle name - Full display name
вҖў Car ID - Technical identifier
вҖў Preview image - Vehicle thumbnail (on hover)
вҖў Action buttons - Copy ID, Get UV Map

Card features:
вҖў Click to view details
вҖў Hover for enlarged preview
вҖў Quick-copy ID to clipboard
вҖў Direct UV map extraction
вҖў Add to project button (in context)

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“җ Getting UV Maps
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

What are UV maps?
UV maps show how the 2D texture wraps around the 3D vehicle model. They are essential templates for creating accurate skins.

Using "Get UV Map":
1. Find your vehicle in Car List
2. Click the "Get UV Map" button
3. Tool extracts the original texture
4. Save to your working directory
5. Open in image editor as reference layer

Best practices for UV maps:
вҖў Use as non-printable guide layer
вҖў Lock the layer to prevent editing
вҖў Set layer opacity to 30-50%
вҖў Design your skin on layers above
вҖў Keep UV map for future reference

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“қ Copy ID Feature
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Why copy ID?
вҖў Ensures exact ID accuracy
вҖў Prevents typos
вҖў Speeds up workflow
вҖў Critical for file naming
вҖў One-click convenience

Using Copy ID:
1. Click the "Copy ID" button
2. ID copied to clipboard
3. Paste into file name
4. Or paste into notes/document
5. Prevents manual transcription errors

Example usage:
Vehicle: Gavril Roamer
Copy ID вҶ’ "roamer"
Create file: roamer_skin_OffRoad.dds

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺЁ Preview Images
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Hover preview system:
вҖў Hover over any vehicle card
вҖў Preview appears after 0.5 seconds
вҖў Shows vehicle render
вҖў Displays vehicle name and ID
вҖў Helps verify the correct vehicle

Preview features:
вҖў High-quality vehicle renders
вҖў Default vehicle configuration
вҖў Default color scheme
вҖў Clear identification
вҖў Quick visual reference

Missing previews:
вҖў Some vehicles may not have previews
вҖў Shows placeholder image
вҖў Does not affect functionality
вҖў ID and name still correct
вҖў Can still create skins normally

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ”Ө Vehicle Name vs. Car ID
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Understanding the difference:

Vehicle Name (Display Name):
вҖў How the vehicle appears in-game
вҖў Human-readable format
вҖў Can contain spaces
вҖў Example: "Gavril Grand Marshal"
вҖў Used for communication

Car ID (Technical Name):
вҖў Internal game identifier
вҖў Machine-readable format
вҖў No spaces, lowercase
вҖў Example: "fullsize"
вҖў Used in file naming

Critical rule:
Always use the car ID in file names, never the display name!

вң“ CORRECT: fullsize_skin_Police.dds
вң— WRONG: Gavril Grand Marshal_skin_Police.dds

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

вҖў Miramar вҶ’ miramar

Others:

вҖў Wendover вҶ’ wendover

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’Ў Tips and Tricks
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Workflow efficiency:
вҖў Use search to quickly find vehicles
вҖў Copy ID immediately when you start working
вҖў Extract UV map before designing

Accuracy checks:

вҖў Double-check spelling

вҖў Create folders per vehicle

howto_content.add_vehicle_content:
Using the Add Vehicle Tab

The Add Vehicle tab lets you add custom, modded or newly released vehicles to BeamSkin Studio. This is important for creating skins for vehicles not found in the default list.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҡ пёҸ CRITICAL: Getting the Car ID
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

The car ID is THE MOST IMPORTANT piece of information. If it is wrong, nothing will work.

Required method - BeamNG console:

1. Launch BeamNG.drive
2. Load any map (Free Roam works best)
3. Spawn the vehicle you want to add
   вҖў ESC > Vehicle menu
   вҖў Select your custom/modded vehicle

4. Open the console
   вҖў Press the ~ key (tilde)

5. Find the exact car ID
   вҖў Look for: "Vehicle replaced: [carid]"
   вҖў The text after "replaced:" is your car ID
   вҖў Example: "Vehicle replaced: gavril_barstow"
   вҖў Copy or write this down EXACTLY

6. Verify the ID
   вҖў Must be exactly as shown
   вҖў Usually lowercase
   вҖў May contain underscores
   вҖў No spaces
   вҖў Case-sensitive!

Console message examples:
"Vehicle replaced: civetta_scintilla" вҶ’ Use: civetta_scintilla
"Vehicle replaced: etk800" вҶ’ Use: etk800
"Vehicle replaced: custom_mod_car" вҶ’ Use: custom_mod_car
"Vehicle replaced: rally_car_2024" вҶ’ Use: rally_car_2024

вҡ пёҸ NEVER guess the car ID!
вҡ пёҸ NEVER use the vehicle's display name!
вҡ пёҸ ALWAYS use exactly what the console shows!

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“Ғ Finding Required Files
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

You need TWO files from the mod folder:
1. JSON file (material definitions)
2. JBEAM file (skin configuration)

Where to find them:

For installed mods:
1. Navigate to your BeamNG mods folder:
   Windows: C:\Users\[username]\AppData\Local\BeamNG.drive\current\mods
   Linux: ~/.local/share/BeamNG.drive/current/mods
   macOS: ~/Library/Application Support/BeamNG.drive/current/mods

2. Find your mod's .zip file
3. Extract ZIP to a temporary folder
4. Navigate to the extracted folder:
   [ModName] вҶ’ vehicles вҶ’ [vehiclename]

5. Look for these files in the vehicle folder:
   вҖў skin.materials.json (or materials.json)
   вҖў main.jbeam (or [vehiclename].jbeam)

For stock game vehicles (Default install):
Windows:
C:\Program Files (x86)\Steam\steamapps\common\BeamNG.drive\content\vehicles\

Linux:
~/.steam/steam/steamapps/common/BeamNG.drive/content/vehicles/

macOS:
~/Library/Application Support/Steam/steamapps/common/BeamNG.drive/content/vehicles/

Then: [vehiclename] folder

Typical file structure:
```
[ModName].zip
в”ңв”Җв”Җ vehicles/
в”Ӯ   в””в”Җв”Җ [vehiclename]/
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ skin.materials.json  вҶҗ You need this
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ main.jbeam          вҶҗ You need this
в”Ӯ       в”ңв”Җв”Җ other files...
```

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“қ Required JSON File
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Common names:
вҖў skin.materials.json (most common)
вҖў materials.json
вҖў [vehiclename]_materials.json
вҖў skin_materials.json

What to look for:
вң“ File is in the vehicle's main folder
вң“ Contains "materials" in the name
вң“ Is a .json file
вң“ Usually 1-50 KB in size
вң“ Contains material definitions for skins

How to verify the right file:
1. Open in a text editor (Notepad, VS Code)
2. Should contain entries like:
   вҖў "paint1"
   вҖў "paint2"
   вҖў "skin"
   вҖў Material properties
3. Contains color/texture definitions

Example JSON content:
```json
{
  "paint1": {
    "mapTo": "paint",
    "color": [1, 1, 1, 1]
  }
}
```

вҡ пёҸ Common mistakes:
вң— Selecting vehicle.json instead
вң— Selecting info.json
вң— Files from wrong folder
вң— Missing "materials" in filename

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ”§ Required JBEAM File
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Common names:
вҖў main.jbeam (most common)
вҖў [vehiclename].jbeam
вҖў [vehiclename]_main.jbeam
вҖў skin.jbeam

What to look for:
вң“ File is in the vehicle's main folder
вң“ Usually named "main.jbeam"
вң“ Is a .jbeam file
вң“ Usually larger than JSON (5-500+ KB)
вң“ Contains vehicle configuration

How to verify the right file:
1. Open in text editor
2. Should contain "jbeam" references
3. Contains vehicle part definitions
4. May have skin-related entries
5. JSON-like structure with vehicle data

Example JBEAM structure:
```json
{
  "main": {
    "information": {
      "name": "Vehicle Name"
    }
  }
}
```

вҡ пёҸ Important notes:
вҖў Some mods have multiple .jbeam files
вҖў If unsure, "main.jbeam" is usually correct
вҖў File should be in the vehicle root folder
вҖў Not in subfolders like "parts" or "slots"

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вһ• Adding a Vehicle - Step by Step
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Complete process:

Step 1: Get car ID from BeamNG console
вҖў Launch BeamNG.drive
вҖў Spawn the vehicle
вҖў Open console (~)
вҖў Find "Vehicle replaced: [carid]"
вҖў Copy the exact ID

Step 2: Find mod files
вҖў Navigate to mods folder
вҖў Extract mod ZIP (if needed)
вҖў Find: vehicles вҶ’ [vehiclename]
вҖў Identify skin.materials.json
вҖў Identify main.jbeam

Step 3: Open the Add Vehicle tab
вҖў Go to Add Vehicle tab in BeamSkin Studio
вҖў Form will be empty and ready

Step 4: Enter car ID
вҖў Paste the EXACT car ID from console
вҖў No changes
вҖў Case-sensitive
вҖў Example: "custom_rally_car"

Step 5: Enter display name
вҖў This is what you will see in menus
вҖў Can be anything you want
вҖў Spaces allowed
вҖў Example: "Custom Rally Car 2024"

Step 6: Select JSON file
вҖў Click "Browse" next to JSON field
вҖў Navigate to vehicle folder
вҖў Select skin.materials.json
вҖў Verify correct file

Step 7: Select JBEAM file
вҖў Click "Browse" next to JBEAM field
вҖў Same folder as JSON
вҖў Select main.jbeam
вҖў Verify correct file

Step 8: Add preview image (Optional)
вҖў Click "Browse" next to image field
вҖў Select a .jpg or .jpeg image
вҖў 400x400+ pixels recommended
вҖў Displayed on vehicle card
вҖў Not required but nice to have

Step 9: Add the vehicle
вҖў Review all information
вҖў Click the "Add Vehicle" button
вҖў Wait for processing
вҖў Check for success message

Success indicators:
вң“ "Vehicle added successfully" message appears
вң“ Vehicle appears in Car List
вң“ Available in Project tab
вң“ Preview image shows (if added)
вң“ Ready to create skins for it

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҡҷпёҸ What Happens Automatically
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

BeamSkin Studio processes your files:

JSON processing:
вҖў Identifies skin material entries
вҖў Removes color palette system
вҖў Updates material paths
вҖў Standardizes material names
вҖў Preserves important properties
вҖў Creates clean skin template

JBEAM processing:
вҖў Finds main skin configuration
вҖў Updates skin entry names
вҖў Sets placeholder values
вҖў Preserves vehicle structure
вҖў Ensures compatibility

File management:
вҖў Creates vehicle folder structure
вҖў Copies processed files
вҖў Generates metadata
вҖў Adds to vehicle database
вҖў Links preview image
вҖў Saves permanently

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вң… Verification Steps
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

After adding vehicle:

1. Check the Car List tab
   вҖў Your vehicle should appear
   вҖў Search for it by name
   вҖў Verify car ID is correct
   вҖў Preview image shows (if added)

2. Test in Project tab
   вҖў Click "Select vehicle"
   вҖў Find your new vehicle
   вҖў Add it to a project
   вҖў Verify it displays correctly

3. Create test skin
   вҖў Make a simple solid color test skin
   вҖў Name file: [carid]_skin_Test.dds
   вҖў Add to project
   вҖў Generate mod
   вҖў Test in BeamNG.drive

If test succeeds:
вң“ Car ID is correct
вң“ Files processed correctly
вң“ Ready for production skins
вң“ Can create full skin sets

If test fails:
вҖў Check car ID matches console exactly
вҖў Verify JSON/JBEAM were correct files
вҖў Enable debug mode to see processing
вҖў Try adding again with correct files

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’Ў Pro Tips
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

File organization:
вҖў Extract mod ZIP to organized folders
вҖў Keep structure: [ModName] вҶ’ vehicles
вҖў Label folders clearly
вҖў Easy to find files later

Preview images:
вҖў Screenshot vehicle in-game
вҖў Crop to square aspect ratio
вҖў Save as high-quality JPEG
вҖў Makes vehicle easy to identify
вҖў Professional appearance

Test strategy:
1. Add vehicle
2. Create simple test skin (solid color)
3. Generate small mod
4. Test in BeamNG.drive
5. If it works, proceed with detailed skins
6. If not, check car ID and files

Documentation:
вҖў Keep notes about custom vehicles
вҖў Record car IDs for reference
вҖў Note mod source
вҖў Document any special requirements
вҖў Helpful for future updates

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҡ пёҸ Common Issues
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

"Skins don't work after adding vehicle":
вҶ’ Car ID must be EXACT from console
вҶ’ Verify you spawned the correct vehicle
вҶ’ Check for typos or capitalization differences
вҶ’ Check console message again

"Can't find JSON or JBEAM files":
вҶ’ Must be in vehicle's main folder
вҶ’ Not in subfolders
вҶ’ Extract mod ZIP fully
вҶ’ Look for common file names

"Processing error":
вҶ’ Enable debug mode in Settings
вҶ’ Watch the console output for error details
вҶ’ Verify files are not corrupted
вҶ’ Try different files if available

"Vehicle appears but skins don't show":
вҶ’ Car ID verification is critical
вҶ’ Test with simple solid color skin
вҶ’ Check BeamNG console for errors
вҶ’ Ensure mod is enabled in-game

Remember: The car ID must be EXACTLY as shown in the BeamNG.drive console. This cannot be stressed enough вҖ” it is the most common cause of issues!
howto_content.troubleshooting_content:
Troubleshooting Common Issues

This chapter addresses the most common problems and their solutions. Read carefully to resolve issues quickly.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вқҢ Skin Doesn't Appear In-Game
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Checklist:
вҳҗ DDS file name uses correct car ID
вҳҗ File name format: carid_skin_skinname.dds
вҳҗ Mod is enabled in BeamNG Content Manager
вҳҗ BeamNG.drive was restarted after mod was added
вҳҗ Mod ZIP is in the correct mods folder
вҳҗ Vehicle in project matches skin's carid
вҳҗ No typos in file name

Step-by-step fix:
1. Verify car ID
   вҖў Check Car List tab for correct ID
   вҖў Compare with your DDS file name
   вҖў Must match EXACTLY (case-sensitive)

2. Check file name format
   вң“ Correct: etk800_skin_Racing.dds
   вң— Wrong: etk800_Racing.dds
   вң— Wrong: etk 800_skin_Racing.dds
   вң— Wrong: ETK800_skin_Racing.dds

3. Verify mod activation
   вҖў Launch BeamNG.drive
   вҖў ESC > Content Manager > Mods
   вҖў Find your mod in the list
   вҖў Checkbox should be checked
   вҖў If not, click to enable

4. Restart BeamNG
   вҖў Close the game completely
   вҖў Wait 5 seconds
   вҖў Launch again
   вҖў Mods update on restart

5. Check mods folder
   вҖў Navigate to output location
   вҖў Verify ZIP file exists
   вҖў File size should be reasonable (not 0 KB)
   вҖў Try extracting to verify contents

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ–јпёҸ DDS File Issues
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

"File won't open in BeamNG":
вҶ’ Must be DDS format (not PNG, JPG, BMP)
вҶ’ Save in image editor with "Save as DDS"
вҶ’ Verify file extension is .dds

"Compression error":
вҶ’ Use BC3 (DXT5) compression
вҶ’ Or BC1 (DXT1) for solid colors
вҶ’ BC7 if your editor supports it
вҶ’ Check editor's DDS export settings

"Resolution issue":
вҶ’ Must be power of 2 (512, 1024, 2048, 4096)
 1:1 Aspect Ratio:
 вҖў Performance quality: 4096 x 4096
 вҖў Standard quality: 8192 x 8192
 вҖў High quality: 16384 x 16384

 2:1 Aspect Ratio:
 вҖў Performance quality: 4096 x 2048
 вҖў Standard quality: 8192 x 4096
 вҖў High quality: 16384 x 8192

"Texture appears corrupted in-game":
вҶ’ Re-export from source file
вҶ’ Check original is not corrupted
вҶ’ Try different DDS compression
вҶ’ Generate mip maps
вҶ’ Use a different image editor

"Black or white texture":
вҶ’ Check UV map alignment
вҶ’ Verify colors are not pure black/white
вҶ’ Test with bright colors first
вҶ’ Check alpha channel
вҶ’ Try BC3 compression

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ”§ Custom Vehicle Not Working
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Most important: Car ID verification

1. Console method (Required)
   вҖў Load BeamNG.drive
   вҖў Spawn the exact vehicle
   вҖў Open console (~)
   вҖў Look for: "Vehicle replaced: [carid]"
   вҖў Use EXACTLY what is shown

2. Common car ID mistakes
   вң— Guessing the ID
   вң— Using vehicle's display name
   вң— Wrong capitalization
   вң— Adding or removing underscores
   вң— Using spaces

3. File selection issues
   вҖў JSON must be from "skin" folder
   вҖў JBEAM must be from "skin" folder
   вҖў Not from main vehicle folder
   вҖў Not from other subfolders
   вҖў Verify correct files

4. Enable debug mode
   вҖў Settings > Debug Mode
   вҖў Watch processing output
   вҖў Check canonical skin detection
   вҖў Verify JSON/JBEAM processing
   вҖў Look for error messages

5. File structure
   вҖў Ensure files are from correct vehicle
   вҖў Check mod compatibility
   вҖў Verify game version matches
   вҖў Test with stock vehicles first

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“Ұ ZIP File Already Exists Error
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Problem: "A mod with this name already exists"

Solutions:
1. Choose a different name
   вҖў Change mod name in project
   вҖў Use a unique identifier
   вҖў Example: MyCoolSkins_v2

2. Delete existing mod
   вҖў Navigate to mods folder
   вҖў Find old ZIP file
   вҖў Delete or rename it
   вҖў Generate new mod

3. Rename old mod
   вҖў Keep both versions
   вҖў Add "_old" suffix to old file
   вҖў Useful for backups
   вҖў Prevents conflicts

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ–јпёҸ Preview Images Not Showing
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Troubleshooting:
вҖў Hover for 0.5-1 second (not immediately)
вҖў Check image is JPEG format (.jpg/.jpeg)
вҖў Minimum 400x400 pixels recommended
вҖў Image should be in correct folder
вҖў Some vehicles may use placeholder

Solutions:
1. Re-add preview image
2. Use a different image file
3. Check file is not corrupted
4. Verify correct JPEG format
5. Preview is cosmetic only (does not affect skins)

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’ҫ Project File Issues
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

"Can't load project":
вҶ’ Verify .bsproject file is not corrupted
вҶ’ Check file path has not changed
вҶ’ Ensure DDS files still exist in original locations
вҶ’ Try opening in text editor to check format
вҶ’ Recreate project if necessary

"Missing skin files":
вҶ’ Tool checks if DDS files exist
вҶ’ Move files back to original location
вҶ’ Or browse again to new location
вҶ’ Update file paths in project
вҶ’ Save project after fixing

"Project won't save":
вҶ’ Check folder write permissions
вҶ’ Ensure sufficient disk space
вҶ’ Verify folder path is valid
вҶ’ Try a different save location
вҶ’ Check antivirus is not blocking

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
вҡҷпёҸ Generation Errors
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

"Mod generation failed":

Check:
вҳҗ All DDS files exist and are accessible
вҳҗ File names follow correct format
вҳҗ No special characters in mod name
вҳҗ Output folder exists and is writable
вҳҗ Sufficient disk space
вҳҗ No files are locked by other programs
вҳҗ At least one vehicle has a skin

Common causes:
вҖў Missing DDS files (moved or deleted)
вҖў File naming errors
вҖў Permission issues
вҖў Disk space
вҖў Files open in other programs
вҖў Invalid mod name

Solutions:
1. Verify all files exist
2. Check file permissions
3. Close other programs
4. Free up disk space
5. Select a different output folder
6. Restart BeamSkin Studio

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

howto_content.advanced_content:
Advanced Topics

This chapter covers advanced techniques and best practices for professional skin creation.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺЁ Advanced Texture Techniques
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Multi-layer workflow:
вҖў Base color layer
вҖў Detail/decal layer
вҖў Aging/dirt layer
вҖў Scratch/damage layer
вҖў Gloss/reflection consideration

Layer organization:
1. Background (UV reference - locked, 30% opacity)
2. Base color paint
3. Main design elements
4. Secondary details
5. Text/logos
6. Aging effects
7. Final color adjustments

Smart workflow techniques:
вҖў Use smart objects for reusable logos
вҖў Preserves quality when resizing
вҖў Easy updates across multiple skins
вҖў Non-destructive editing

Color management:
вҖў Use adjustment layers for global changes
вҖў Non-destructive color grading
вҖў Easy to create color variations
вҖў Maintain consistent color schemes

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“җ UV Mapping Optimization
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Understanding UV density:
вҖў Hood/doors: High detail (large UV space)
вҖў Roof/trunk: Medium detail
вҖў Undercarriage: Low detail (small UV space)
вҖў Mirrors/trim: Minimal detail

Detail distribution:
вҖў Focus details where most visible
вҖў Simplify less visible areas
вҖў Balance file size against quality
вҖў Consider common viewing angles

Seam management:
вҖў Identify UV seams on the template
вҖў Avoid placing key details over seams
вҖў Use clone stamp to blend seams
вҖў Test in-game to verify seamless appearance

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ”§ Technical Optimization
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

File size management:
вҖў Use appropriate DDS compression
вҖў Don't unnecessarily upscale textures
вҖў Generate proper mip map chains
вҖў Balance visual quality against performance
вҖў Test on various hardware

Performance considerations:
вҖў Standard quality (2048ВІ) for most users
вҖў High quality (4096ВІ) for enthusiasts
вҖў Consider multiplayer impact
вҖў Optimize for VR if applicable

Quality assurance checklist:
вҳҗ Test on multiple graphics settings
вҳҗ Verify in different lighting conditions
вҳҗ Check at various viewing distances
вҳҗ Test in rain and night conditions
вҳҗ Validate on different monitor types
вҳҗ Ensure text/numbers are readable

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ’Ў Professional Tips
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Creating skin collections:
вҖў Develop cohesive themes
вҖў Maintain consistent design language
вҖў Use shared color palettes
вҖў Standardize detail level
вҖў Professional naming conventions

Version control:
вҖў Semantic versioning (1.0, 1.1, 2.0)
вҖў Maintain changelogs
вҖў Archive old versions
вҖў Document updates clearly

Community best practices:
вҖў High-quality preview images
вҖў Clear installation instructions
вҖў Credit used resources
вҖў Respond to user feedback
вҖў Regular updates and bug fixes
howto_content.faq_content:
Frequently Asked Questions

Quick answers to the most commonly asked questions.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺЁ Skin Creation
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Q: What software do I need?
A: Any image editor that supports DDS (Paint.NET free, GIMP free, Photoshop paid).

Q: Can I use PNG or JPG?
A: No, must be DDS format for BeamNG.drive.

Q: What resolution should I use?
A1: for aspect ratio 1:1: 8192x8192 (standard) or 16384x16384 (high quality).
A2: for aspect ratio 2:1: 8192x4096 (standard) or 16384x8192 (high quality).
Note: Resolutions must be power of 2 (512, 1024, 2048, etc).

Q: Do I need the UV map?
A: Helpful but not required. Use "Get UV Map" in Car List for templates.

Q: Can I use the same skin on multiple vehicles?
A: No, each vehicle has unique UV mapping. Create separate skins per vehicle.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ”§ Tool Usage
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Q: Can I add multiple skins to a vehicle?
A: Yes! Add the vehicle once, then add unlimited skins.

Q: Can a mod contain multiple vehicles?
A: Yes! That's the main feature вҖ” one mod for all your skins.

Q: How do I update an existing mod?
A: Change mod name or remove old mod, then generate again.

Q: Do project files work on different computers?
A: Yes, but DDS paths must be accessible or re-browsed.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹ“Ғ Files and Naming
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Q: What is a car ID?
A: Technical vehicle identifier (e.g. "etk800"). Find it in the Car List tab.

Q: Is the car ID case-sensitive?
A: Yes! Must match exactly. Use the Copy ID button for accuracy.

Q: Can file names have spaces?
A: No! Format: carid_skin_SkinName.dds (no spaces anywhere).

Q: What happens if I misspell the car ID?
A: The skin won't work. Always copy from the Car List tab.

в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ
рҹҺ® BeamNG Integration
в”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғв”Ғ

Q: Where is my mod installed?
A: The default Steam mods folder or the custom location you selected.

Q: Do I activate my mod?
A: Yes! Content Manager > Mods > Enable checkbox.

Q: Why can't I see my skins?
A: Check mod is enabled, BeamNG has been restarted and car ID is correct.

Q: Do mods work in BeamMP?
A: No, custom mods are single-player only.
